steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what 'anticipating' most nearly means as used in the text. The Mole is 'eagerly anticipating the moment when he would be at home again' — he hasn't arrived yet, and the word 'eagerly' signals strong positive desire toward that future moment.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Describing: The Mole isn't narrating or depicting the moment — he desires it. ✗
  • Choice B — Getting ahead of: This means surpassing or outpacing something, not longing for a future event. ✗
  • Choice C — Looking forward to: Captures both the forward-looking orientation and the eager desire the text signals. ✓
  • Choice D — Instructing: Completely unrelated to the context of the Mole's emotional state. ✗
That matches Choice C — Looking forward to.
